is_p_falciparum.evidence: The paper concerns HIV-1, DARC, and mentions Plasmodium vivax malaria resistance via the DARC-negative phenotype; it does not study P. falciparum.

reports_target_markers.evidence: The only genetic marker studied is the host DARC T-46C (rs2814778) SNP and human chemokine gene polymorphisms (CCL5, CCR5), not any P. falciparum drug-resistance codon.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Frequencies reported are for human DARC genotypes in HIV+/HIV- and HGDP-CEPH populations, not parasite drug-resistance loci.

sub_country_location.evidence: Samples are from the WHMC cohort (San Antonio, TX, USA) and the HGDP-CEPH worldwide DNA set; no P. falciparum sampling.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: No malaria parasite collection is described; human cohort with no relevant temporal binning for parasite markers.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human subjects are African Americans (US) and HGDP-CEPH samples; no African field/clinical parasite isolates.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study combining in vitro HIV experiments and genetic-epidemiologic cohort analysis.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Not a malaria parasite dataset; irrelevant to duplication concerns for this database.

supplement.evidence: Supplementary material concerns admixture score details; no P. falciparum marker data would be present.

assessment.summary: This is a study of the host DARC T-46C polymorphism and its effect on HIV-1 acquisition and progression, with in vitro HIV trans-infection experiments. Plasmodium is mentioned only as background (DARC as the P. vivax receptor). It contains no P. falciparum drug-resistance marker data, no African field parasite isolates, and no target codon frequencies. Entirely out of scope.
exclusion_reasons: Not a P. falciparum study (concerns HIV-1 and host DARC genetics)
No curated drug-resistance markers or codon frequencies reported
No African P. falciparum field/clinical isolates
No parasite collection location or time data
